crisply from 48px favicon to 512px.

commits.subject: fix(icon): convert dm lettermark from text to outlined paths
commits.body:
SVG <text> elements depend on fonts being available at render time.
Sharp rasterizes server-side and may not have Arial Black installed,
causing fallback rendering or blank output. Replaced with hand-drawn
filled paths that render identically in all environments.

Fixed "d" bowl direction (stem on right, bowl opens left) — previous
path version read as "bm" because the stem was on the left like a "b".

Tags: icon, branding, build
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: header-styling
Semver: patch

commits.title: convert dm lettermark from text to outlined paths

commits.subject: feat(icon): replace document icon with "dm" lettermark
commits.body:
Old icon was a detailed document on dark navy — unreadable at 28px in the
header and looked like a broken image. New icon is a bold "dm" lettermark
in cyan on transparent background. Works in both light and dark mode.

Removed unused PWA icons (adaptive-icon, splash-icon, icon.png) — this
is a static portfolio site, not a PWA. Bumped header display to 32px.

commits.subject: feat(nav): replace inline nav links with burger menu
commits.body:
Moved Projects/Tools/Patterns links, theme toggle, and Save as PDF into
a disclosure-pattern burger dropdown. Header is now just logo + title +
burger icon — cleaner on all screen sizes.

Burger menu uses aria-expanded (not role="menu") per WAI-ARIA disclosure
pattern. Closes on backdrop click, Escape key, and item click. Theme
toggle stays open for quick toggling. iOS Safari backdrop click handled
via cursor-pointer. Focus management: first item on open, trigger on close.

Tags: ui, nav, accessibility

commits.subject: fix(badges): consistent domain + data taxonomy for project cards
commits.body:
Replaced inconsistent badges (mixed tech-type and domain) with a
two-badge system: primary badge = domain (Finance, Design, Knowledge,
etc.), subtle badge = data characteristic (On-device, Cloud, Anonymous).
On-device highlights privacy — data never leaves the browser. Anonymous
highlights synctone's core feature. Model-pear has no data badge (stateless).

commits.subject: fix(ci): update Node to 22, fix audit vulnerability, update description
commits.body:
- CI Node version: 20 -> 22. Vite 7.3.1 requires ^20.19.0 || >=22.12.0.
  Node 20 in CI may resolve to a version below 20.19.0, breaking the
  build. Node 22 matches local dev environment.
- npm audit fix: resolved high-severity picomatch vulnerability
  (transitive dep of Vite, build-time only, not exploitable at runtime
  but keeps CI clean)
- Updated package.json description to match current project purpose

Tags: ci, deps, security
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 3
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: portfolio-site
Semver: patch

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Bh93JoHZAKz7zY48tGvfZ1
commits.title: update Node to 22, fix audit vulnerability, update description

commits.subject: fix(security): escape all dynamic HTML content, normalize URLs
commits.body:
Security:
- All meta.json values rendered into HTML are now escaped via
  escapeHtml() — badge, repo, privacy labels/values, use cases,
  liveUrl, repoUrl, and markdown table headers. Prevents XSS if
  meta.json ever contains user-controlled or malicious content.
- escapeHtml already handles &, <, >, " (added in previous commit)

Consistency:
- Normalized all Vercel URLs to include trailing slash — few-lap,
  synctone, sun-sea-o were missing it in both index.html and
  meta.json. All 12 live URLs now consistently end with /

Tags: security, xss, urls, cleanup
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: portfolio-site
Semver: patch

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Bh93JoHZAKz7zY48tGvfZ1
commits.title: escape all dynamic HTML content, normalize URLs

commits.subject: fix(bugs): JS bugs, visual issues, focus states, print improvements
commits.body:
Bugs fixed:
- Event listener accumulation: Tab click handlers were added inside
  render(), accumulating on every re-render. Switched to event
  delegation on document (one listener handles all tabs)
- escapeHtml missing quote escaping: Added " — prevents broken
  HTML attributes if meta.json contains quotes
- Copy button selector too broad: querySelector('.doc-action-btn')
  always selected the first button. Now uses getElementById('copy-btn')

Visual fixes:
- Status dot colors: Hardcoded #22c55e/#a1a1aa replaced with
  var(--pico-primary)/var(--pico-muted-color) for dark mode
- Logo border-radius: Hardcoded 6px replaced with
  var(--pico-border-radius) for consistency
- Logo href: Dead anchor (#) replaced with ./ (home link)

Accessibility:
- Added :focus-visible outlines to card-links, doc-tabs, and
  doc-action-btn — keyboard navigation now shows focus indicators

Print:
- Added break-inside: avoid to table and pre elements — prevents
  code blocks and tables from splitting across pages

Tags: fix, bugs, accessibility, visual, print
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: portfolio-site
Semver: patch

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Bh93JoHZAKz7zY48tGvfZ1
commits.title: JS bugs, visual issues, focus states, print improvements

commits.subject: fix(html): remove all inline styles from project.html
commits.body:
Replaced 8 inline style attributes with proper CSS classes:
- Loading state: style="text-align..." → .loading-state class
- Project hero: style="margin-bottom..." → .project-hero class
- Title row: style="display:flex..." → .project-title-row class
- Description: style="color:..." → .project-description class
- Actions: style="margin-top..." → .project-actions class
- Private label: style="cursor:default..." → .private-label class

Both HTML files now have zero inline styles. All presentation is in
CSS classes within the <style> block (project.html) or styles.css.

Tags: cleanup, html, css

commits.subject: fix(mobile): touch targets, safe area insets, grid overflow, active states
commits.body:
Mobile UX fixes identified during user/design perspective review:

- Touch targets: Icon buttons increased from 36px to 44px (2.75rem),
  card links get padding for 44px+ tap area, doc tabs and action
  buttons get min-height: 2.75rem — all meet Apple HIG 44px minimum
- Safe area insets: Added viewport-fit=cover to both pages and
  env(safe-area-inset-*) to body, nav, and footer — prevents content
  overlap with iPhone notch/Dynamic Island
- Grid overflow: Lowered card-grid minmax from 300px to 280px and
  card-grid-sm from 260px to 250px — prevents 2-column overflow on
  576-640px screens where Pico's container leaves ~560px available
- Active states: Added :active opacity to icon buttons, card links,
  article cards, and doc action buttons — provides touch feedback
  since :hover doesn't fire on mobile
- Dynamic meta description: project.html now sets <meta description>
  from meta.json after load — improves SEO when sharing project links

Tags: mobile, ux, touch, accessibility, seo
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: portfolio-site
Semver: patch

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Bh93JoHZAKz7zY48tGvfZ1
commits.title: touch targets, safe area insets, grid overflow, active states

commits.subject: fix(html): replace <nav> card-links with <div> to avoid Pico nav conflicts
commits.body:
Pico CSS applies padding, spacing, and flex layout to all <nav>
elements. Using <nav> for card action links (Details, Live app,
Source) incorrectly received Pico's nav styling. Changed to <div>
since these are action links within cards, not site navigation.

Also cleaned up styles.css:
- Removed section margin override (let Pico handle spacing)
- Removed redundant hero/print padding overrides
- Added !important to .icon-btn overrides (Pico uses high-specificity
  selectors on <button> elements, setting width: 100% by default)
- Merged duplicate .card-grid > article selectors
- Documented why card-links must be <div> not <nav>

Tags: fix, pico-css, html, accessibility
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: portfolio-site
Semver: patch

commits.subject: feat(design): integrate Pico CSS Jade theme with Space Grotesk + Inter fonts
commits.body:
Complete restyle of the portfolio site:

- Pico CSS v2 Jade theme imported via Vite from node_modules (not CDN)
  for consistent build pipeline and no external runtime dependency
- Space Grotesk (headings) + Inter (body) via Google Fonts with preconnect
- Switched dark mode from .dark class to data-theme attribute — Pico uses
  [data-theme="dark"] for its dark mode selectors
- Updated flash prevention inline scripts on both pages
- Updated theme.js toggle, cross-tab sync, and OS preference tracking
- Restructured HTML to Pico's semantic conventions: <nav> for header,
  <article> for cards with <header>/<footer>, proper list structure
- Rewrote styles.css as override layer — removed all base typography,
  colors, focus states (Pico handles these). Only card grids, badges,
  icon buttons, and print overrides remain as custom CSS
- Added color-scheme meta tag for browser chrome

Tags: design, pico-css, fonts, dark-mode, restyle
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: portfolio-site
Semver: minor

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Bh93JoHZAKz7zY48tGvfZ1

commits.subject: fix(theme): remove meta theme-color suggested implementation
commits.body:
The Meta Theme-Color section suggested dynamically switching theme-color
between background colors on dark mode toggle. This caused invisible
status bar text when the OS color scheme opposed the app theme. Removed
the entire section, the dynamic update from the useDarkMode hook, and
the related Key Lessons. Projects should handle theme-color on their own
terms, not follow a flawed pattern.

Tags: pwa, theme, dark-mode, documentation, cleanup

Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid

commits.body:
The brand color is constant — no JS update needed on dark mode toggle.
The HTML meta tag is the single source of truth. Removes dead code that
previously switched between background colors and was the root cause of
the status bar visibility bug.

commits.subject: fix(theme): use brand color for PWA status bar instead of background colors
commits.body:
The dark mode implementation replaced the branded status bar color with
generic background colors (#ffffff/#1a1a2e), causing visibility issues
when the OS color scheme opposed the app theme (light text on light
background, or dark text on dark background). The status bar is a
branding surface — a mid-tone brand color (#10b981) provides contrast
for status bar text in both light and dark OS modes.

Tags: pwa, theme, dark-mode, status-bar, documentation

Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: theme-dark-mode

commits.subject: docs(claude-md): add Theme & Dark Mode to Suggested Implementations
commits.body:
Add comprehensive Theme & Dark Mode pattern covering:
- CSS variable palette with semantic tokens (raw hex, v3/v4 compatible)
- Tailwind dark mode config for both v3 (darkMode: 'class') and v4
  (@custom-variant dark)
- useDarkMode web hook with localStorage persistence, system preference
  fallback, safe storage access (try/catch for sandboxed iframes)
- Flash prevention inline script in index.html (same early-capture
  pattern as PWA beforeinstallprompt)
- Dual meta theme-color tags with media queries
- React Native useAppTheme context with AsyncStorage persistence
- themeHydrated flag to prevent splash screen hiding before stored
  preference loads (fixes flash bug found in few-lap audit)
- color-scheme: dark on html.dark for native form elements
- Print override forcing light colors
- Related patterns: currentColor SVGs, chart theme-awareness,
  content themes vs app dark mode distinction
- 13 Key Lessons covering all edge cases and gotchas

Findings from double-check audit:
- localStorage needs try/catch (SecurityError in sandboxed iframes)
- theme() function deprecated in Tailwind v4 (use raw hex)
- textTertiary contrast values were swapped (fixed to pass WCAG AA)
- AsyncStorage hydration flash in React Native (hold splash screen)
- No CSS transitions on switch (industry standard: instant)
- CSP inline script caveat documented
- Android PWA manifest theme_color override documented

Tags: documentation, ui, dark-mode, accessibility, cross-project
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: neutral
Epic: cross-project-alignment
Semver: minor

https://claude.ai/code/session_01X9TF1L5bgUD8cYTLjQXYeY
commits.title: add Theme & Dark Mode to Suggested Implementations

commits.subject: docs(claude-md): add Burger Menu and discontinued repos to Suggested Implementations
commits.body:
Add comprehensive Burger Menu pattern to Suggested Implementations covering:
- WAI-ARIA disclosure pattern (not role="menu") with rationale
- React (Vite + Tailwind) variant with full accessibility
- React Native (Expo) variant with cross-platform support
- Z-index scale for consistent stacking across all projects
- Standard menu items table (Help, PWA, Auth categories)
- iOS Safari backdrop click fix (cursor-pointer)
- Focus management with hasBeenOpenRef guard
- Scroll lock via overscroll-contain (avoids double-lock)
- RN Web stopPropagation fix (e.target === e.currentTarget)
- Tailwind v3/v4 dark mode compatibility notes
- 10 Key Lessons documenting all edge cases and gotchas

Also add AI Note marking plant-fur and coin-zapp as discontinued repos
to skip in cross-project operations.

commits.subject: fix(icons): improve SVG rendering with 400 DPI and geometricPrecision
commits.body:
Sharp now rasterizes the SVG at 400 DPI (~5.5x the default 72 DPI
coordinate space) before downscaling to target sizes. Combined with
shape-rendering="geometricPrecision" on the SVG root element, this
produces noticeably crisper edges — especially on the 192px PWA icon.

CLAUDE.md updated with both techniques in the generator script example,
SVG design rules, and key lessons sections.

Tags: icons, svg, rendering, sharp

Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid

Semver: patch

commits.subject: docs(pwa): add timer leak and install prompt race condition fixes to PWA guidelines
commits.body:
Two common PWA bugs documented as implementation guidelines:
1. Timer leaks on unmount — nested setTimeout callbacks survive cleanup when only
   the outer timeout is cleared. Fix: track all timeout IDs or use a mounted ref guard.
2. Install prompt race condition — beforeinstallprompt fires before SPA framework mounts
   on repeat visits. Fix: inline script in HTML captures event early, hook consumes it
   with useState initializer, useEffect fallback covers first-visit case.

Tags: documentation, pwa
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: pwa-guidelines
Semver: patch
commits.title: add timer leak and install prompt race condition fixes to PWA guidelines

commits.subject: docs(readme): document repo purpose and usage
commits.body:
Explains that glow-props is a static file host for serving
fetchable config files (like CLAUDE.md) to Claude Code sessions
across projects via GitHub Pages.

Tags: docs, readme
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: low
Debt: neutral
Epic: developer-tooling
Semver: patch

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Ffm42hU9KHVqbYk3XPaQDk
commits.title: document repo purpose and usage

commits.subject: chore(config): add global Claude rules file to repo
commits.body:
Provides a deliverable CLAUDE.md with process, principles, code
standards, documentation guidelines, AI notes, and prohibitions
that can be applied across all projects.

Tags: config, ai-rules, standards
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: low
Debt: neutral
Epic: developer-tooling
Semver: minor

commits.subject: Add GitHub Pages deployment workflow
commits.body:
Uses the official GitHub Pages actions to deploy on push
to main. Requires Pages source set to "GitHub Actions" in
repo settings.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Ffm42hU9KHVqbYk3XPaQDk
commits.title: Add GitHub Pages deployment workflow

commits.subject: Add static site for fetchable text delivery
commits.body:
Set up project structure with index.html landing page,
texts/ directory for content, and a sample hello.txt file.
Deployable to GitHub Pages or any static host.
